name: Shift4 Payments, Inc.
business: Shift4 is a leading independent provider of software and payment processing solutions in the U.S., and a leader in tax-free shopping. The company sells integrated payment processing, point-of-sale systems, eCommerce platforms, and business intelligence tools to businesses ranging from small local merchants to multinational enterprises across industries such as restaurants, hospitality, venues, retail, and e-commerce.

exposures.sentence: We are a leading independent provider of software and payment processing solutions in the U.S. and we are expanding our payment processing solutions to international markets.
Shift4Shop Our turnkey eCommerce platform provides everything a merchant needs to build their business online.
The Giving Block Our cryptocurrency donation marketplace allows donors to easily gift a variety of cryptocurrencies to approximately 2,400 charities.

For example, we use machine learning, artificial intelligence and automated decision-making technologies in our SkyTab Website Builder, a tool powered by artificial intelligence for restaurants to quickly and easily create a website for their business.
As a result of the acquisition of Global Blue Group Holding AG (Global Blue) in the third quarter of 2025 (Global Blue Merger) we are also a leader in tax-free shopping (TFS).
Our payments platform includes market-leading security features that help prevent consumer card data from entering the merchant’s environment.

catalysts.event: Expected closing of acquisition of Worldline's North American subsidiaries
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, we entered into exclusive negotiations to acquire Worldline’s North American subsidiaries for approximately $84 million of cash, subject to customary closing adjustments. The contemplated transaction is expected to close in the first quarter of 2026, subject to customary approvals.

catalysts.event: Amendment to Credit Facilities reducing interest rate margins
catalysts.sentence: On January 5, 2026, the Borrower entered into an Amendment No. 3 to its Second Amended and Restated First Lien Credit Agreement (the Amended Credit Agreement) with GS and the lenders party thereto. As a result of the Amendment No. 3, the applicable interest rate margin over the relevant benchmark rate on the Borrower’s term loans has been, (a) in the case of term loans that bear interest with reference to the term SOFR, reduced from 2.50% per annum under the Credit Agreement to 2.00% per annum under the Amended Credit Agreement and (b) in the case of alternate base rate loans, reduced from 1.50% per annum under the Credit Agreement to 1.00% per annum under the Amended Credit Agreement.
